message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: DSRT CLUB (Desert Club) (La Quinta)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 250 reviews; Tripadvisor ~11 reviews (ranked #56 of 127 La Quinta restaurants); Google review count [UNVERIFIED]
- Sentiment: Mostly positive with some recent polarization — strong praise for food (octopus, jambalaya, lamb), cocktails, and decor; recent 1-star reviews (Feb 2026) cite reservation/hostess issues and noise
- Longevity: Opened 2023 (under 3 years operating); owners Chris and Anita Chmielak previously opened sister restaurant RD RNNR in 2019 — ownership appears stable
- Credentials: ["Featured in Desert Health News (deserthealthnews.com)","Listed in Palm Springs Life directory","Featured in Old Town La Quinta dining directory","Listed by City of La Quinta visitor site (playinlaquinta.com)"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces for: 'supper club Coachella Valley', 'Old Town La Quinta dining', 'date night La Quinta', 'fine dining La Quinta'. Does NOT consistently surface for broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'fine dining Palm Desert' queries. Surfaces strongly when 'supper club' is the explicit term — owns that niche regionally alongside The Purple Room (Palm Springs).
- Site structured data: Weak — desertclublq.com homepage lacks schema.org markup (no JSON-LD/microdata/RDFa). Menu page links to PDF files rather than machine-readable HTML menu. Hours present in human-readable form but not structured. OpenTable integration provides some agent-readable signal but the owned site itself is thin for agent consumption.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.prompt: Look at the raw HTML source. Is there any JSON-LD (script type="application/ld+json"), schema.org microdata (itemtype attributes), or RDFa markup? Report exactly what structured data markup is present or absent. Also note if the menu links to PDFs and if hours are in structured form.

input.query: DSRT CLUB La Quinta supper club Chris Anita Chmielak RD RNNR

content.content:
# Structured Data Analysis: DSRT CLUB Website

## Structured Markup Assessment

**JSON-LD:** Absent - No `<script type="application/ld+json">` tags detected.

**Schema.org Microdata:** Absent - No `itemtype` attributes found in the HTML.

**RDFa Markup:** Absent - No RDFa properties identified.

## Additional Observations

**Menu Links:** The navigation includes a "Menu" link, but the provided HTML doesn't reveal the destination format. No PDF reference is visible in this excerpt.

**Hours Format:** Operating hours are presented as plain text only: "Lunch Thursday - Sunday (ONLY) 11:30 - 2:00" and "Dinner Sunday - thursday 4:30 - 8:30 / Friday & saturday 4:30 - 9:00." They lack structured formatting (no schema.org `openingHoursSpecification` or similar markup).

## Summary

The website contains **zero structured data markup**. Business information (hours, contact details, menu) exists only in human-readable text, making it invisible to search engines and other automated systems that parse semantic HTML.

Links: [{"title":"DSRT Club Delights with Classic Flare and Clean Cuisine - Desert Health®","url":"https://deserthealthnews.com/story/dsrt-club-delights-with-classic-flare-and-clean-cuisine/"},{"title":"RD RNNR - Issuu","url":"https://issuu.com/gcvchamber/docs/lq_gem_june_web/s/25935304"},{"title":"About – DSRT CLUB","url":"https://desertclublq.com/about/"},{"title":"DSRT CLUB - Updated June 2026 - 378 Photos & 250 Reviews - 78075 Main St, La Quinta, California - Supper Clubs - +17607777424 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/dsrt-club-la-quinta-2"},{"title":"City of La Quinta Economic Update Includes New Shops, Restaurants | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/vision/city-of-la-quinta-economic-update-includes-new-shops-restaurants/"},{"title":"DSRT Club - Old Town La Quinta","url":"https://oldtownlaquinta.com/dsrt-club/"},{"title":"DSRT CLUB | La Quinta CA | Facebook","url":"https://www.facebook.com/DSRTCLUB/"},{"title":"DSRT CLUB, La Quinta - Restaurant Reviews, Photos & Reservations - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32584-d27094525-Reviews-DSRT_Club-La_Quinta_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"DSRT CLUB Restaurant - La Quinta, CA","url":"https://www.opentable.com/r/dsrt-club-la-quinta"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about DSRT CLUB, the La Quinta supper club, and its owners:

## Overview
DSRT Club was the vision of owners Chris and Anita Chmielak who opened RD RNNR, another east valley favorite, in 2019. The executive chef is James Nellis, who is working on a more upscale menu – like you might find at an old school supper club.

## History and Inspiration
DSRT CLUB is named after the historic Desert Club that was the happening hangout in La Quinta decades ago. This iconic establishment graced the desert landscape from the late 1930s until its closure in the late 1980s, and it was during the 1940s and 1950s that The Desert Club truly shone as a symbol of swanky elegance in the desert.

## Location and Details
DSRT Club is located at 78075 Main Street in Old Town La Quinta serving lunch Wednesday – Sunday 11:30am – 2pm and dinner nightly 5-9pm. Reservations are recommended by calling (760) 777.7424.

## Menu
Chef James Nellis curated the chef-driven menu describing it as California coastal rustic, with uniquely prepared seafood such as twice-cooked octopus, hibiscus seared Maine scallops, and poppyseed crusted albacore arriving fresh daily.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about DSRT CLUB in La Quinta based on Yelp and other review sources:

## Overview
DSRT CLUB is a thoughtfully curated adult dining experience paying tribute to the original back in 1937, and was established in 2023.

## Reviews Summary
The restaurant has received generally positive reviews across multiple platforms:

**Ambiance & Decor**: DSRT Club in La Quinta Old Town offers a fantastic dining experience with an amazing atmosphere, friendly staff, and delicious food. It pays homage to the 1930s OG eatery, Desert Club, with throwback vibes like checkerboard tiles and palm-patterned wallpaper, and has an alluring contemporary feel with swanky additions such as neon signs and marble textiles.

**Food & Drinks**: The menu features standout items such as the unbelievably tender octopus and crispy skin branzino paired with a rich romesco sauce and oven-roasted cauliflower. The curated drinks are unique and exceptionally crafted.

**Service**: Customers appreciate the attentive service, with staff members who are knowledgeable and eager to guide diners through the menu.

**Reservations**: Reservations are highly recommended as tables tend to fill quickly due to the restaurant's popularity and exceptional dining experience.

Based on my search, here are some of the best restaurants in Coachella Valley for 2026:

## Popular Restaurant Picks

Top restaurants in Coachella include Red Falcon, El Tranvia Restaurant, Papa Headz, Kitchen Ten Eleven, The Coachella Firehouse Bar And Grill, Indio Taphouse, TKB Bakery & Deli, Jalisco Restaurant, Taproom 29, and Babe's Smokehouse & Tavern.

## Fine Dining Options

For upscale dining experiences, several excellent establishments stand out:

- The Purple Room Supper Club in Palm Springs offers a unique blend of fine dining and live entertainment, known for its sophisticated ambiance and classic American cuisine.

- Pacifica Seafood Restaurant in Palm Desert is known for its fresh catches and extensive wine list, offering a sophisticated yet relaxed dining experience with a range of seafood options, from oysters to Chilean sea bass.

- The Edge Steakhouse, located at The Ritz-Carlton in Rancho Mirage, offers a premier dining experience with stunning views of the Coachella Valley, specializing in prime steaks and fresh seafood.

- Morgan's in the Desert at La Quinta Resort & Club offers a refined farm-to-table dining experience with a menu inspired by the flavors of the American Southwest.

## Diverse Cuisine

Miro's Restaurant in Palm Springs is family-owned and offers a unique blend of Mediterranean and Eastern European cuisine, featuring dishes such as Hungarian goulash and grilled lamb chops.

Based on the search results, here are some excellent supper club and fine dining options in the Coachella Valley and La Quinta area for a date night:

## Top Supper Club Options:

**DSRT Club** (Old Town La Quinta) - With its low lighting, leather banquettes, and polished bar, DSRT Club revives the supper-club spirit in Old Town La Quinta. Paying homage to the OG eatery, Desert Club, from the 1930s, it features checkerboard tiles and palm-patterned wallpaper exuding nostalgic throwback vibes, while swanky additions like neon signs and marble textiles create an alluring and contemporary feel. The kitchen commemorates classics while adding modern California flair, with enviable entrees and shareables including brown butter lobster tail and hibiscus-seared Maine scallops.

**The Purple Room Supper Club** (Palm Springs) - This venue offers a unique blend of fine dining and live entertainment as a nod to the mid-century supper clubs of old Hollywood, known for its sophisticated ambiance and classic American cuisine, allowing diners to enjoy expertly prepared dishes while taking in a live show.

## Fine Dining at La Quinta Resort:

**Morgan's in the Desert** - A cornerstone of La Quinta Resort & Club since 1926, Morgan's in the Desert pairs historic elegance with a modern refresh, offering California-driven, seasonal cuisine, classic and Prohibition-era cocktails, live piano music and inviting indoor-outdoor spaces.

**Twenty6 at La Quinta Resort & Club** - This restaurant blends historic charm with modern American bistro dining, named for the resort's 1926 opening, and serves seasonal, chef-driven cuisine complemented by craft cocktails and inventive mocktails.

These establishments offer the perfect combination of ambiance, dining quality, and entertainment for a memorable date night in the Coachella Valley.

Links: [{"title":"DSRT Club - Old Town La Quinta","url":"https://oldtownlaquinta.com/dsrt-club/"},{"title":"DSRT CLUB – Elevated Dining","url":"https://desertclublq.com/"},{"title":"DSRT CLUB - Updated June 2026 - 378 Photos & 250 Reviews - 78075 Main St, La Quinta, California - Supper Clubs - +17607777424 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/dsrt-club-la-quinta-2"},{"title":"DSRT CLUB Restaurant - La Quinta, CA","url":"https://www.opentable.com/r/dsrt-club-la-quinta"},{"title":"DSRT CLUB | City of La Quinta","url":"https://www.playinlaquinta.com/restaurants/dsrt-club/"},{"title":"Menu – DSRT CLUB","url":"https://desertclublq.com/menu-2/"},{"title":"DSRT CLUB, La Quinta - Restaurant Reviews, Photos & Reservations - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32584-d27094525-Reviews-DSRT_Club-La_Quinta_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"A Dining Guide to Old Town La Quinta That’ll Make Your Mouth Water","url":"https://localemagazine.com/old-town-la-quinta-dining-guide/"},{"title":"DSRT Club Delights with Classic Flare and Clean Cuisine - Desert Health®","url":"https://deserthealthnews.com/story/dsrt-club-delights-with-classic-flare-and-clean-cuisine/"},{"title":"DSRT CLUB Menu (Updated 2026) - Wheree","url":"https://dsrt-club.wheree.com/menu"}]

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about DSRT CLUB and its inclusion in Old Town La Quinta dining directories:

DSRT CLUB is a true inspiration of classic vintage Desert dining tucked away in the heart of Old Town La Quinta. The restaurant is featured in dining guides, paying homage to the original Desert Club from the 1930s that once catered to upscale patrons as a glamorous supper club.

DSRT CLUB is included in The Ultimate Dining Guide to Old Town La Quinta, with a menu that draws inspiration from La Quinta's iconic heritage as a celebrity hotspot. The establishment has been highlighted in multiple dining directories and publications as a notable dining destination in Old Town La Quinta.

DSRT Club is located at 78075 Main Street in Old Town La Quinta serving lunch Wednesday – Sunday 11:30am – 2pm and dinner nightly 5-9pm.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about DSRT Club reservation and hostess complaints:

One TripAdvisor review describes a negative experience where a diner made dinner reservations two weeks in advance and received three confirmation emails, but upon arriving on time was told there would be a 30-minute wait, and the hostess was very condescending. The reviewer left for another restaurant and stated they would not return despite being local residents who had visited the Desert Club multiple times in the past.

However, the search results also show that DSRT Club generally receives positive reviews. The restaurant is praised for its fine dining experience, culinary expertise, attentive service, and unique cocktails, and visitors have praised the decor, food, drinks, and service.

So while there is at least one documented hostess complaint regarding condescending behavior and reservation handling, the overall reception appears to be largely positive based on recent reviews.

content.text: I have enough to verify. Let me finalize my assessment.

confirms: Yelp review count of 250 (Yelp listing shows 378 Photos & 250 Reviews as of June 2026)
Site structured-data assessment: desertclublq.com has zero JSON-LD/microdata/RDFa; hours are plain text only — confirmed via direct fetch of HTML source
Owned by Chris and Anita Chmielak who previously opened RD RNNR in 2019 (Desert Health News article confirms)
Opened 2023 (under 3 years), modeled on 1930s-era Desert Club
Featured in Desert Health News, Old Town La Quinta dining directory (oldtownlaquinta.com/dsrt-club/), and listed on City of La Quinta visitor site (playinlaquinta.com/restaurants/dsrt-club/)
Surfaces strongly for 'supper club Coachella Valley' / 'date night La Quinta' / 'Old Town La Quinta dining' queries — explicitly mentioned alongside The Purple Room as the supper-club niche
Does NOT surface in broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley 2026' results (lists feature Red Falcon, Purple Room, Pacifica, Edge Steakhouse, Morgan's — not DSRT)
Recent reservation/hostess complaint exists on Tripadvisor (condescending hostess, 30-min wait despite confirmed reservation)
Menu page exists at desertclublq.com/menu-2/ (separate URL, consistent with non-structured menu approach)
contradicts: Tripadvisor ranking claim is wrong: claimed '#56 of 127 La Quinta restaurants' — actual Tripadvisor data shows DSRT Club ranked #69 of 108 La Quinta restaurants with 4.0/5 rating and 11 reviews. Review count (~11) is correct, but rank and denominator are both off.

Palm Springs Life 'directory' listing is overstated: the Palm Springs Life hit found is an economic-update article mentioning new La Quinta restaurants, not a curated directory listing. Reframe as 'mentioned in Palm Springs Life editorial coverage' rather than 'listed in Palm Springs Life directory'.
flagged_as_unverified: Google review count (already flagged unverified in claim — remains so)
Specific Feb 2026 1-star reviews citing noise (reservation/hostess complaint confirmed generically, but exact Feb 2026 timing and noise-specific complaints not independently dated)

Visibility gap score of 6 (judgmental score; evidence supports a gap in broader regional queries and weak owned-site schema, consistent with mid-range gap, but the exact numeric value is not independently verifiable)
input.notes: Core narrative holds: DSRT Club owns the regional 'supper club' niche, has zero owned-site structured data (confirmed by direct HTML fetch — no JSON-LD, microdata, or RDFa; hours are plain text), and fails to surface in broad 'best Coachella Valley restaurants' lists. Two factual errors to correct: (1) Tripadvisor rank is #69 of 108, not #56 of 127; (2) Palm Springs Life appearance is editorial mention, not a directory listing. Yelp review count, ownership history, and credential set otherwise check out.
